decision.reason: The stock CK-Tile grouped GEMM example requires tile-aligned M/N/K and pads each S2048 expert group to M=128. It is correct for tile-aligned BF16 shapes and reaches high TFLOPs on padded work, but the 4.0x row-padding tax leaves it slower than the retained max-count padded torch.bmm(out=) baseline for the isolated W2 dgrad slot.

tile_aligned_smoke.note: The same binary failed validation for M=32 or N=64 because the stock configs have kPadM=false and kPadN=false.

read: This closes the stock CK-Tile example as a direct replacement for S2048 W2 dgrad. The useful CK follow-up is not another example run; it is a custom CK/CK-Tile binding/config with smaller M tiles or explicit M padding support below 128.
